key_insight: TO-Master uses an LLM agent to automate the full topology optimization workflow—from model setup to solver execution and postprocessing—using natural language instructions plus optional geometry, mesh, or image inputs.
engineering_relevance: It lowers the implementation barrier for finite-element topology optimization, making compliance, thermal, and stress-constrained design studies more accessible to non-experts and faster to deploy in engineering workflows.
method: LLM agent orchestrates computational tools rather than doing optimization directly
Automatically builds/checks FEM TO models, boundary conditions, and typed solver arguments
Supports generated/uploaded meshes, image-to-mesh conversion, 2D/3D problems, multiple loads, and engineering geometries
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on benchmark and engineering examples, plus an instruction ablation study showing tool-use rules, reasoning guidance, and few-shot examples improve robustness under ambiguous input.
watch_item: Reliability still depends on correct tool orchestration and input interpretation, so ambiguous user requests or invalid geometry/mesh setups may require human review.
paper_title: TO-Master: an LLM-agent framework for automated topology optimization
